Title: Informal Armchair Arrangement, Sidewalk
Caption: Photographic record of improvised domestic seating arrangement on sidewalk.
Observations: A pair of upholstered armchairs and a small table are positioned on a rug directly within the sidewalk, forming a temporary domestic zone in public space. The grouping imports the visual and social language of the living room into the street, offering comfort, pause, and conversation where movement would normally dominate. Its placement destabilizes the boundary between private interiority and public thoroughfare, making domestic space briefly visible as an improvised urban condition.

Title: Child-Sized Chair, Private Residence
Caption: Photographic record of child-sized side chair in domestic interior.
Observations: This child-sized side chair reproduces the formal language of adult domestic furniture in miniature. Its carved crest rail, upright back, and upholstered check seat soften the object’s disciplinary function by lending it familiarity. Scale operates as the primary regulatory device, training posture and placement through proportion and introducing the child to domestic codes of order, restraint, and presentation.

Title: Decorative Side Chair, Outdoor Market
Caption: Photographic record of side chair with figural needlepoint-style upholstery in market setting.
Observations: The chair’s needlepoint-style upholstered seat shifts emphasis from comfort to visual narrative. Before it is encountered as seating, it presents itself as decorated object. In the market setting, domestic ornament becomes commodity, and the act of sitting is momentarily deferred by the demand to look.
Map_Label: SS-003 — Decorative Side Chair, Outdoor Market

Title: Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
Caption: Photographic record of ceremonial upholstered seating within the Louvre’s Napoleon III Apartments.
Observations: Ceremonial upholstered seating preserved within the Napoleon III Apartments at the Louvre, a Second Empire interior created in 1861 for Napoleon III’s Minister of State. Designed for elite reception rather than informal rest, the chair participates in a larger decorative system in which gilding, upholstery, and controlled spacing organize visibility and social order. In the museum setting, its original function survives as atmosphere and historical display while bodily use is suspended.
Map_Label: SS-004 — Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior

Title: Modified Armchair — Exhibition Installation
Caption: Photographic record of modified seating incorporated into an exhibition installation.
Observations: A modified armchair is presented within an exhibition setting, where the addition of human hair destabilizes the boundary between furniture, body, and sculptural object. The familiar language of seating remains legible, but comfort is interrupted by a material treatment that suggests grooming, accumulation, and intimate residue. Rather than supporting the body directly, the chair stages sitting as a withheld possibility and redirects attention toward display, performance, and ritualized care.

Title: Painted Ladder-Back Chair — Commercial Interior
Caption: Photographic record of painted wooden seating observed in a commercial interior.
Observations: A small red chair is positioned within a retail environment, where its cracked painted finish and worn surface foreground prolonged use and material aging. The object remains legible as functional seating, yet its visual presence exceeds its practical scale. Within the shop, it contributes to an atmosphere shaped by reuse, accumulation, and the display of domestic objects as both utility and character.
Map_Label: SS-006 — Painted Ladder-Back Chair — Commercial Interior

Title: Upholstered Armchair — Roadside (Winter)
Caption: Photographic record of upholstered seating observed roadside during winter.
Observations: Domestic upholstered seating is displaced into unmanaged public transit space. Environmental conditions temporarily suspend use, rendering comfort hypothetical rather than accessible. The object remains structurally intact but environmentally compromised.

Title: Artist’s Step Chair — Improvised Seating
Caption: Photographic record of artist’s step chair used as improvised seating.
Observations: Designed to support standing reach rather than seated rest, the object is pressed into improvised sitting. The body compensates for inadequate scale and support, remaining active rather than settled. Duration is structurally limited.
Map_Label: SS-008 — Artist’s Step Chair — Improvised Seating

Title: Reclining Loungers — Hospitality Lawn
Caption: Photographic record of leisure seating aligned for guest use.
Observations: A continuous row of reclining loungers arranged in parallel, facing a shared view. Spacing and orientation are uniform. Although designed for rest, the seating prescribes how leisure is performed—alignment, duration, and relational positioning are structured. Comfort is abundant but organized.

Title: Ornamental Armchairs — Storefront Threshold
Caption: Photographic record of ornamental seating used as storefront display.
Observations: Two ornate chairs frame the threshold between street and interior. Elevated scale and decorative excess suggest ceremonial authority rather than rest. Although positioned in public space, the chairs do not invite occupation. Seating operates symbolically—signaling luxury and hierarchy—while regulating who may cross the threshold and under what posture.

Title: Student Desk-Chair — Classroom Synchronization
Caption: Photographic record of classroom seating during instructional time.
Observations: The desk-chair integrates seating and work surface into a single unit, limiting bodily repositioning. Orientation is fixed toward the front of the room, aligning attention collectively. Scale and rigidity restrict lateral movement; comfort is secondary to endurance. Seating functions as a synchronizing device, holding multiple bodies in parallel states of instructional readiness.

Title: Lakeside Armchairs — Horizon-Oriented Rest
Caption: Photographic record of lakeside balcony seating.
Observations: Seating oriented toward a fixed horizon encourages sustained stillness. The body is permitted to remain without instruction, endpoint, or display.

Title: Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement
Caption: Photographic record of dining chairs displaced from domestic interior to curbside exterior.
Observations: A matched set of dining chairs positioned outdoors after removal from their intended interior setting. Designed for shared domestic gathering, they remain oriented toward collective use while temporarily suspending it. The arrangement preserves social possibility, but shifts it into a provisional condition between abandonment, pickup, and reassignment.
Map_Label: SS-013 — Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement

Title: Lounge Chair — Exhibition Proxy for Rest
Caption: Photographic record of seating presented for viewing rather than use.
Observations: Presented as seating but withdrawn from bodily use, the chair operates as a visual proxy for rest. Its form suggests comfort while denying occupation, integrating into the exhibition’s viewing apparatus. The object becomes something to be looked at rather than inhabited.
Map_Label: SS-014 — Lounge Chair — Exhibition Proxy for Rest

Title: Bentwood Armchair — Retail Seating
Caption: Photographic record of seating that invites pause without encouraging duration.
Observations: A bentwood armchair positioned within a retail environment, offering brief support without encouraging extended occupation. The lightweight frame and cane surfaces suggest permeability and airflow, reinforcing short-duration use. The form accommodates pause during browsing rather than sustained rest, balancing invitation with subtle temporal limitation.

Title: Molded Plywood Lounge Chair — Sidewalk Resale Display
Caption: Photographic record of molded plywood seating displayed for resale.
Observations: A molded plywood lounge chair positioned within a sidewalk resale display. Though structurally capable of supporting extended rest, its placement among stacked and adjacent furniture withdraws it from immediate use. The chair operates as a commodity image rather than a seating instrument, inviting visual evaluation instead of bodily occupation. Comfort is implied but suspended pending purchase.
Map_Label: SS-016 — Molded Plywood Lounge Chair — Sidewalk Resale Display

Title: Deep-Seated Armchair — Domestic Rest
Caption: Photographic record of deep-seated armchair in active domestic use.
Observations: A deep-seated upholstered armchair positioned within a private interior and shown in active use. Its form supports extended rest and asymmetrical bodily orientation, allowing the occupant to recline and elevate the legs without maintaining formal posture. Unlike display or transitional seating, the chair accommodates duration, privacy, and release, functioning as a domestic structure for sustained comfort rather than regulation.

Title: Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
Caption: Photographic record of ceremonial salon seating in the Napoleon III Apartments, Louvre Museum.
Observations: Ceremonial upholstered seating preserved within the Napoleon III Apartments, a largely intact Second Empire interior created in 1861 for Napoleon III’s Minister of State. The chairs organize the room through repetition, ornament, and controlled spacing, producing a social field oriented toward reception rather than relaxation. In the museum setting, their original function survives as image and atmosphere while bodily use is suspended.
Map_Label: SS-018 — Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
